Risk Scenario: Compromise of RMM platform enables attacker to push malware or commands to multiple customer environments
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Weak privileged access governance, excessive permissions, limited monitoring
Proposed Primary Controls: MFA, PAM, least privilege, logging, vendor hardening

Risk Scenario: Phishing steals employee credentials and grants access to M365, VPN, ticketing or vault
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Inconsistent MFA, limited awareness training, weak conditional access
Proposed Primary Controls: MFA, phishing training, conditional access, email protection

Risk Scenario: Leak of customer documentation exposes network diagrams, admin details and security architecture
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Poor data classification, broad access to documentation, excessive sharing
Proposed Primary Controls: Access control, classification, DLP, review of sharing permissions

Risk Scenario: Ransomware disrupts internal operations and support services
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Endpoints exposed to phishing/malware, backups not isolated or tested consistently
Proposed Primary Controls: EDR, immutable backups, restore testing, segmentation

Risk Scenario: Abuse of privileged accounts causes unauthorized changes in customer or internal systems
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Shared or over-privileged accounts, poor session control, inadequate review
Proposed Primary Controls: Named admin accounts, PAM, access reviews, session logging

Risk Scenario: Backup failure prevents recovery after cyber incident or hardware loss
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Backups incomplete, untested restores, credentials tied to production domain
Proposed Primary Controls: 3-2-1 backups, separate creds, monthly restore tests

Risk Scenario: Lost or stolen device exposes sensitive company or customer data
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Missing full-disk encryption, no MDM enforcement, local data storage
Proposed Primary Controls: BitLocker/FileVault, MDM, remote wipe, screen lock

Risk Scenario: Unpatched vulnerability on workstation or server leads to compromise
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Inconsistent patching, limited maintenance windows, no compliance dashboard
Proposed Primary Controls: Patch management process, vulnerability scanning, maintenance calendar

Risk Scenario: Flat internal network allows lateral movement from user device to critical systems
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Insufficient segmentation between office, admin, lab and guest networks
Proposed Primary Controls: VLAN segmentation, firewall rules, admin jump host

Risk Scenario: Business email compromise or domain spoofing leads to invoice fraud or reputation damage
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Weak email authentication and poor verification of payment change requests

Risk Scenario: GDPR or contractual non-compliance due to inadequate policies and evidence
Key Vulnerability / Cause: No formal ISMS baseline, incomplete records of processing, weak governance
Proposed Primary Controls: Policy pack, risk register, supplier controls, retention rules

Risk Scenario: Supply chain compromise through SaaS vendor, distributor portal or MSP tooling
Key Vulnerability / Cause: No formal vendor assessment, excessive trust in third parties
Proposed Primary Controls: Vendor security review, security clauses, scoped integrations

Risk Scenario: Support tickets or shared files expose sensitive customer data to unauthorized staff
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Role model too broad, no classification labels, limited audit review
Proposed Primary Controls: RBAC, confidentiality labels, logging, approvals for exports

Risk Scenario: Incident goes undetected or is escalated too late, breaching SLA and increasing impact
Key Vulnerability / Cause: No 24/7 monitoring, alert fatigue, missing incident runbooks
Proposed Primary Controls: Alert tuning, IR plan, severity matrix, designated on-call roles

Risk Scenario: Remote access session hijack or misuse compromises customer systems
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Password-based access, limited IP/device restrictions, no step-up auth
Proposed Primary Controls: MFA, device trust, session timeout, dedicated admin path

Risk Scenario: Lab or test environment is used as a pivot into production or customer data
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Poor isolation, reused credentials, outdated test systems

Risk Scenario: Insider misuse or error causes data loss, unauthorized disclosure or service outage
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Broad access, weak joiner/mover/leaver process, little accountability

Risk Scenario: Extended outage of cloud business platforms disrupts sales, support and billing
Key Vulnerability / Cause: Single-provider dependency, no fallback procedures, limited export capability
